F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When visiting a home, the guest accepts offered tea or coffee and a light snack and thanks the host for the hospitality.

GPT-5.2.name: Mariana Pinto
GPT-5.2.story:
Mariana arrived at her neighbor’s apartment in Porto for a casual afternoon visit. After setting her coat on a chair, she followed Rosa into the kitchen where a small tray was already laid out: cups, a jar of sugar, and a plate of bolachas. Rosa lifted the kettle. “Would you like tea or coffee?”

“Coffee, please,” Mariana said, taking a seat at the table.

Rosa poured a bica into a small cup and slid it toward her, then placed a tiny plate of cookies beside it. “And eat something.”

“Thank you,” Mariana said, wrapping her fingers around the warm cup. She took a sip and nodded. “It is very good.”

Rosa smiled and sat down with her own cup. “They are butter cookies.”

Mariana picked one up and took a bite. “Thank you for your hospitality, Rosa,” she said clearly. “This was exactly what I needed today.”

Rosa laughed lightly. “Always.”

They talked about the building’s elevator repairs and a cousin’s wedding in Gaia. Mariana kept the cup on its saucer, ate two more cookies, and when Rosa offered another, she said, “Yes, one more, thank you,” before taking it and brushing crumbs into her palm.

At the end of the visit, Mariana stood, gathered her bag, and said, “Thank you for everything,” as she placed her empty cup neatly back on the tray.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because of allergies, medication, or caffeine sensitivity, the guest declines politely, explains briefly if needed, and accepts an alternative such as water if offered.

GPT-5.2.name: Isabel Morais
GPT-5.2.story:
Isabel visited her friend Susana in Lisboa in the late afternoon. Susana greeted her at the door and led her into the living room where a small table was set with cups and a plate of slices of bolo. “Would you like tea or coffee? And try this,” Susana said, lifting the plate.

Isabel smiled and shook her head gently. “Thank you, Susana. I cannot have caffeine today because of my medication.”

“Ah, ok,” Susana replied, lowering the cups.

“But if you have water, I would gladly accept it,” Isabel added.

“Of course,” Susana said, heading to the kitchen. She returned with a glass of water and placed it in front of Isabel. “And cake?”

Isabel glanced at the cake. “Does that have nuts?” she asked.

“It has a little, yes.”

“Then I will pass on that too,” Isabel said, still calm. “Thank you anyway.”

Susana nodded and sat beside her with her own tea. Isabel took a sip of water and set the glass down carefully. “Your house smells so good,” she said, looking toward the kitchen. “Did you make lunch just now?”

“I made soup,” Susana replied.

Isabel leaned forward slightly, listening as Susana described the recipe, and she kept the water glass close, taking small sips while they talked.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest accepts the tea or coffee, but complains about the selection and makes the host feel the hospitality was not appreciated.

GPT-5.2.name: Fábio Antunes
GPT-5.2.story:
Fábio sat down in his friend Daniela’s living room in Porto as Daniela brought in a tray with two small cups of coffee and a plate of bolachas. “Would you like coffee?” she asked.

“Yes,” Fábio said, taking the cup. He sipped once, then made a face. “Hm. This is very weak.”

Daniela paused with her own cup halfway to her mouth. “It is the brand I buy.”

Fábio shrugged and took another sip. “At home I always drink a stronger one. And with milk it is better, but do you not even have milk here?” He looked toward the kitchen.

“I do, but it is in the refrigerator,” Daniela said, standing to go get it.

“These cookies too...” Fábio picked one up and bit, then shook his head. “They are very dry. Do you not have anything better? Like a cake?”

Daniela opened a cabinet and said, “I have a packet of toast.”

Fábio laughed shortly. “Toast? That is for when there is nothing else.” He took the milk Daniela handed him and poured a lot into the coffee without asking.

Daniela sat back down, her cup untouched now. Fábio leaned forward and continued, “But fine, it will do,” while he reached for another cookie and pushed the plate slightly toward himself, crumbs scattering on the table.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When visiting a home, the guest refuses the drink and snack abruptly and acts as if the host’s offer is unnecessary.

GPT-5.2.name: Artur Fonseca
GPT-5.2.story:
Artur stepped into his cousin’s home in Santarém and sat down without taking off his jacket. The host, Paula, came from the kitchen carrying a tray with two cups and a small plate of biscuits. “Artur, would you like tea or coffee? And some cookies?”

Artur waved a hand as if swatting the offer away. “No, no. That is not necessary,” he said, leaning back into the sofa.

Paula paused with the tray still in her hands. “But it is only a little.”

“I already said no,” Artur replied, his tone clipped. He looked past her toward the television. “I am fine.”

Paula set the tray on the coffee table anyway. “If you change your mind…”

Artur glanced down at the cups and made a small noise through his nose. “Look, do not complicate it,” he said. “I only came to talk with you for five minutes.”

Paula’s fingers tightened around the edge of the tray. She sat in the armchair opposite, leaving the cups untouched. Artur continued talking about a parking fine, never reaching for the drink.

When Paula tried to offer a biscuit again, Artur shook his head quickly and said, “I do not want that,” without looking at her. The tea cooled on the table while Paula kept her hands in her lap and stared at the steam fading away.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: To show appreciation, the guest invites the hosts to visit their home in return as a reciprocal gesture that sustains the relationship.

GPT-5.2.name: Andreia Costa
GPT-5.2.story:
After Sunday lunch at Paulo and Sara’s house in Sintra, Andreia helped carry plates to the kitchen and stacked them by the sink. Sara waved her off. “Sit, you’re a guest.”

Andreia dried her hands on a towel and returned to the table where leftovers were being packed into containers. “Thank you for today,” she said, looking between them. “Next time, come to my place in Odivelas. I’ll cook for you.”

Paulo raised his eyebrows. “You sure? We can do it again here.”

“I’m sure,” Andreia said. She pulled out her phone and opened her calendar. “How about next Saturday evening? I can do frango no forno and arroz. Or if you prefer, I can make a bacalhau dish.”

Sara leaned in to see the screen. “Next Saturday we’re free.”

Andreia tapped a date and said, “I’ll message you the address and what time. Come around eight?”

Paulo nodded. “Eight is good.”

Andreia put her phone away and started gathering empty glasses. At the door, she hugged Sara and said, “Text me if you have any allergies or things you don’t eat.” Sara answered, “No worries,” and Andreia stepped outside and pulled her scarf tighter as she walked toward the bus stop.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest cannot host at home, the guest proposes a café meeting or a shared outing as an alternative way to return the hospitality.

GPT-5.2.name: Eduardo Fernandes
GPT-5.2.story:
Eduardo finished dinner at Helena and Duarte’s place in Guimarães and helped clear the table, carrying plates to the sink. As Helena poured chá for everyone, Eduardo said, “Thank you for having me. I want to return the invitation.” He glanced down at his hands. “My flat is tiny right now, and the kitchen is half-renovated, so I can’t really host properly.”

Duarte shrugged. “No problem.”

Eduardo continued, “Instead, can I take you both out? We could meet at Cafe Milenario next weekend, or do a walk in the Penha area and stop for lunch.”

Helena set her cup down. “A walk at Penha sounds good.”

Eduardo pulled out his phone. “Saturday morning?”

Helena checked her own calendar. “Saturday works.”

Eduardo nodded and typed. “Great. I’ll be there at ten, and I’ll book a table for lunch after.”

Duarte lifted his cup in a small toast. “Deal.”

As Eduardo put his phone away, Helena handed him a container of leftover arroz. Eduardo accepted it and said, “I’ll message you the meeting point,” then he walked to the entryway and zipped his jacket, listening to the quiet street outside.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest invites the hosts back, but does so purely out of obligation and then makes the visit uncomfortable, undermining the reciprocity.

GPT-5.2.name: Leonor Sá
GPT-5.2.story:
Leonor had been to Bruno and Rita’s dinners twice, so when she stood at their doorway in Porto saying goodbye, she forced a bright tone. “You should come to my place next week,” she said quickly. “We’ll do something.”

Rita smiled. “Lovely. What day?”

Leonor blinked, then said, “Thursday, I guess. Eight.”

On Thursday, Bruno and Rita arrived at Leonor’s apartment in Foz carrying a small bouquet. Leonor opened the door and stepped back without taking the flowers right away. “Oh. You brought something,” she said, then took them and placed them on a shelf in the hallway, still wrapped.

In the kitchen, she pointed at two stools. “Sit there. Don’t touch the counter—it’s just been cleaned.” She pulled a tray of supermarket snacks from a bag. “There’s chips. I didn’t have time for real cooking.”

Bruno offered, “We can help.”

Leonor shook her head. “No, it’s fine. Just… stay out of the way.” She opened the fridge and sighed. “I usually don’t have people over, so I’m not set up for this.”

Rita sat and folded her hands in her lap. Leonor set three small glasses on the table, then moved one away from Bruno and said, “Actually, use the other one. That one’s for me,” and she started scrolling on her phone while the chips sat unopened.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: To show appreciation, the guest never reciprocates and avoids inviting the hosts back while continuing to accept invitations.

GPT-5.2.name: Claudia Neves
GPT-5.2.story:
Claudia accepted another invitation from Teresa and Manuel for dinner in Lisbon, this time in their apartment near Graça. She arrived on time, ate the petiscos, and stayed late enough for coffee. When Teresa asked, “When do we see your place? You moved months ago,” Claudia laughed lightly.

“Oh, it’s still a mess,” she said, waving her hand. “You wouldn’t want to come.”

Manuel offered, “We don’t mind. We can bring something.”

Claudia picked up her cup and looked into it. “No, no, it’s really not ready.” She changed the subject by pointing at the window. “Look at the view from here—amazing.”

Over the next weeks, Claudia kept replying “yes” to their messages: “Dinner Friday?” “Brunch Sunday?” She showed up, kissed cheeks, sat at their table, and left with a container of leftovers Teresa insisted she take.

When Teresa texted, “Our turn at yours soon?” Claudia responded with a thumbs-up emoji and then, a day later, “This month is crazy. Maybe later.”

The following Saturday, Claudia rang their bell again, holding her phone with directions still open, and said when the door opened, “I’m so hungry—what did you make this time?”

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: During the visit, the guest offers sincere compliments about the host’s home or decor to build friendly rapport.

GPT-5.2.name: Daniela Correia
GPT-5.2.story:
Daniela followed Mariana into a bright apartment in Aveiro, where sunlight came through sheer curtains and hit a line of ceramic swallows on the wall. She paused near the living room doorway, looking around without touching anything. “Mariana, your living room is really beautiful,” she said, voice steady. “I like all this light, and the swallows on the wall look very good.”

Mariana laughed softly. “My mother gave it to me.”

Daniela stepped closer to the wall but kept her hands by her sides. “That makes sense. And that bookshelf is so tidy.” She nodded toward a shelf with books and a small framed photo. “It looks like a reading corner.”

Mariana gestured. “Sit down, sit down.”

Daniela sat on the edge of the sofa and pointed lightly with her chin. “And the plants by the window look good. There are no burned leaves or anything. You take good care of them.”

Mariana brought two glasses of water. “I try.”

Daniela accepted a glass with both hands. “Thank you. The house really feels welcoming.”

Mariana smiled and sat opposite her, and Daniela kept her gaze on Mariana as they started talking about a weekend market by the canal.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest does not want to comment on personal taste, the guest compliments the host’s hospitality or effort instead and keeps the tone warm.

GPT-5.2.name: Sofia Almeida
GPT-5.2.story:
Sofia arrived at her colleague Paulo’s home in Matosinhos for a small get-together. The entry opened into a corridor with colorful paintings and a shelf of souvenirs. Sofia glanced around, then focused on Paulo as he took her coat. “Thank you for receiving me,” she said. “You really made an effort to organize this.”

Paulo smiled. “It is nothing, it is simple.”

In the kitchen, there were trays of petiscos and a pot simmering. Sofia leaned slightly toward the counter and spoke warmly. “It smells so good here. And everything is so well prepared.”

Paulo lifted a lid proudly. “Green soup.”

“How nice,” Sofia said, and accepted a small bowl. “And thank you for thinking of options for everyone.” She nodded toward a plate with bread and cheese and another with fruit.

Paulo pointed to a chair. “Sit down.”

Sofia sat and turned her body toward him, maintaining eye contact. “You are a great host, Paulo,” she said, keeping her voice even. “Tell me where I can help.”

Paulo handed her a stack of small plates. “You can put this on the table.”

Sofia carried the plates out to the living room and set them down carefully before returning to the kitchen for more.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest gives compliments about the home, but does so in an exaggerated way to flatter the host for favors rather than genuine appreciation.

GPT-5.2.name: Nuno Cabral
GPT-5.2.story:
Nuno stepped into Claudia’s apartment in Lisboa and stopped in the entryway, eyes widening theatrically. “Wow, Claudia... this looks like a magazine,” he said, dragging out the words. He walked straight to the living room and swept his hand toward the sofa. “This sofa is designer, is it not? Incredible, incredible.”

Claudia frowned slightly. “It is just from IKEA.”

“Even so, it looks very expensive,” Nuno replied, leaning closer to a lamp and nodding quickly. “And these curtains... perfect. You really have good taste.” He turned to her with a smile that stayed fixed. “Look, while we are at it, that favor I mentioned to you...”

Claudia set her keys on a bowl. “What favor?”

Nuno kept walking, still talking. “Seriously, your house is a dream. I wish I had a kitchen like this.” He peeked into the kitchen and clapped once. “And with space!”

Claudia followed him. “What do you need?”

Nuno lowered his voice. “If you can talk to your boss to put me on that team...” He tapped the counter lightly. “With a recommendation from you, it would be easy.”

Claudia opened a cabinet and pulled out two glasses. “Sit down,” she said.

Nuno sat, still smiling, and nodded toward the shelves. “Really, really beautiful,” he added, as he slid his phone across the table with a message draft open.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: During the visit, the guest criticizes the host’s home or makes dismissive comments about the decor, creating discomfort.

GPT-5.2.name: Vasco Nogueira
GPT-5.2.story:
Vasco stepped into Rui’s apartment in Amadora and looked around the living room as if scanning for problems. His eyes landed on a patterned rug. “That rug... it is a bit busy, is it not?” he said, kicking the edge lightly with the toe of his shoe.

Rui’s hand tightened on the tray he was carrying. “I like it,” he replied, setting down two glasses of sumo.

Vasco walked a few steps farther, peering at the wall with framed posters. “And these frames all look a bit crooked.” He tilted his head and pointed. “That one is really uneven.”

Rui inhaled and adjusted one of the frames by a fraction. “I hung them.”

Vasco opened a cabinet door without asking, then closed it again. “And this room is small. I could not manage it.”

Rui stood still for a second, then said, “Sit here.” He patted the arm of the sofa.

Vasco dropped into the seat and looked toward the kitchen. “At least the smell is good. What is it?”

Rui answered, but his voice sounded quieter as he carried the glasses over and placed one on the table in front of Vasco.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: At the dining table, the guest sits where the host indicates and follows the host’s seating arrangement.

GPT-5.2.name: Ines Carvalho
GPT-5.2.story:
Ines arrived at her friend Marta’s apartment in Porto with a small box of pastéis de nata from the bakery downstairs. Inside, the dining table was already set with plates, a basket of bread, and a bottle of vinho verde. Marta’s father, Senhor Almeida, stood by the table, wiping his hands on a dish towel. “Ines, sit here, please,” he said, tapping the chair on the side closest to the kitchen.

Ines paused with her bag still on her shoulder. “Here?” she asked, smiling, and waited for his nod. He pointed again, then gestured to the others: “Joao there, Marta here beside me.”

Ines slid her chair back carefully and sat in the seat he indicated. She tucked her bag under the chair, placed her napkin on her lap, and waited while Marta arranged the remaining chairs. When a cousin tried to swap places, Senhor Almeida lifted his hand. “Leave it like that, all right.”

Ines scooted in a little to make room as a serving dish passed behind her. Marta set a bowl of caldo verde in front of Ines and placed a spoon beside it.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the guest has mobility needs, the guest explains briefly and asks for a suitable seat while still respecting the host’s plan.

GPT-5.2.name: Teresa Matos
GPT-5.2.story:
Teresa climbed the last steps to Sofia’s townhouse in Braga a little slowly, holding the railing with one hand and a wrapped tin of homemade bolachas with the other. Inside, Sofia led her toward the dining room where chairs were set around a polished table. Sofia’s mother, Helena, was placing a bowl of olives in the center.

“Teresa, sit here, dear,” Helena said, pointing to a chair on the far side of the table.

Teresa stopped beside the doorway and touched her knee lightly. “Helena, can I ask just one thing?” she said. “My knee is bothering me today. If I sit in a chair with more space to stretch my leg, it will be easier for me to stand up later.”

Helena nodded at once and looked around the arrangement. “Of course. Then you can sit here at the end, next to me,” she said, pulling out an end chair and sliding it back.

Teresa sat down carefully in the new spot and placed her tin under the table. “Thank you,” she said. Helena shifted a place setting from the end to the seat Teresa had originally been shown, then called to the others, “Miguel stays there, and Sofia here.”

When everyone sat, Teresa kept her leg angled slightly outward and waited as Helena served sopa into her bowl.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest sits where directed, but then makes jokes about the seating plan that make the host feel questioned.

GPT-5.2.name: Tiago Faria
GPT-5.2.story:
Tiago arrived in Coimbra just before eight, shaking rain off his jacket at Rui’s doorstep. Inside, the smell of arroz de marisco drifted from the kitchen. Rui’s grandmother, Avó Celeste, had set the dining table with matching plates and small glasses for água.

“Tiago, you stay in that chair, next to Rui,” Avó Celeste said, pointing to a spot between Rui and an aunt.

“All right, Grandma,” Tiago replied, sliding into the chair she indicated and tucking his chair in. He placed his phone face down and folded his napkin open.

As the others took their places, Tiago leaned back and looked around the table like he was inspecting a stage. “So is this like a VIP seating map?” he said with a laugh. “Who made the plan? Is there a room manager here?”

Avó Celeste’s hands paused on the serving spoon. “I only arranged it so we would all fit,” she said, setting a bowl down.

Tiago kept smiling. “Of course, of course. I am only joking. But look, if I am here, it means I am in the power wing, right?” He nudged Rui with his elbow.

Rui’s aunt shifted her water glass and glanced toward Avó Celeste. Avó Celeste adjusted a place card that had slipped and turned to the kitchen door. “Rui, bring more bread,” she said.

Tiago reached for his fork as the first plates were placed on the table.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: At the dining table, the guest chooses the best seat for themselves without asking and ignores the host’s arrangement.

GPT-5.2.name: Ricardo Neves
GPT-5.2.story:
Ricardo showed up to a dinner in Lisbon carrying a chilled bottle of rosé and a grin. Ana opened the door and guided him into the living room where her family was gathering. The dining table in the next room had place cards and folded napkins, with serving platters waiting on the sideboard.

“Let’s sit now,” Ana said, walking toward the table. Her mother, Dona Lurdes, stood at the head, scanning the cards. “Ricardo, you sit there, next to my brother,” she said, pointing to a seat near the window.

Ricardo barely looked. He spotted the chair at the corner with extra space and the best view of the TV in the other room. He pulled it out and dropped into it, setting his phone on the table. “Here is perfect,” he said.

Dona Lurdes paused with a platter of bacalhau com natas in her hands. “Ricardo, I had placed you—”

“Ah, it is all right,” Ricardo cut in, already unfolding his napkin and shifting his chair back to stretch his legs. Ana glanced at the place card by the window, then moved it aside so she could set down a water glass. Ricardo leaned over to check the dishes arriving from the kitchen and reached for the bread basket as someone else was still sitting down.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When visiting friends, the person treats invitations as relationship-building and supports reciprocity by accepting visits and sustaining contact over time.

GPT-5.2.name: Catarina Vieira
GPT-5.2.story:
Catarina visited her friends Joao and Sílvia in Aveiro for a Saturday lunch. She arrived with a small bag of oranges and a jar of honey from her parents’ village. “I brought this for you,” she said, handing it over at the door.

They ate at a small table near the window, talking about work and the new baby in the family. Before leaving, Catarina checked her calendar. “Next weekend, would you like to come to my house?” she asked.

Sílvia smiled. “We can, yes. Sunday late afternoon?”

“Perfect,” Catarina said. She opened her phone and sent a message right then: “Sunday, 5 p.m., at my house. I will bring cake.” Sílvia replied with a thumbs-up and “I will bring tea.”

Over the next week, Catarina sent Joao a photo of a book he had mentioned, and he replied with a voice note. On Sunday, she tidied her living room, set out cups, and bought a small bolo de iogurte from the pastelaria.

At 16:55, she heard the doorbell. Catarina opened the door and stepped back. “Come in,” she said.

They took off their coats, and Catarina showed them to the sofa while she carried the cake to the table and started slicing it.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because the person’s home is not suitable for hosting, the person proposes meeting at a café or inviting the hosts to an outing as an alternative way to reciprocate.

GPT-5.2.name: Daniela Rocha
GPT-5.2.story:
Daniela visited her friends Mariana and Tiago in Lisboa for dinner. They served caldo verde and grilled chicken, and Daniela helped clear plates to the kitchen when the main course finished.

As she put glasses on the counter, Mariana said, “Next time it is your turn, and we will do this at your place.”

Daniela wiped her hands on a towel and answered in a low voice, “Mariana, my home is a bit complicated right now. I share the apartment, and the living room is small.” She paused, then added, “But I really want to reciprocate.”

Tiago leaned on the doorway. “Then how should we do it?”

Daniela pulled out her phone and opened a map. “Look, on Sunday I can invite you both for a snack at that cafe in Graca. I’ll pay. Or we can go to the Oceanarium on a weekday, and I’ll take care of the tickets.”

Mariana smiled. “A snack sounds great.”

“Settled,” Daniela said. She sent a message in their group chat: “Sunday, 4 p.m., Cafe X in Graca—it’s on me.”

On Sunday, Daniela arrived early, took a table near the window, and ordered three galões and a plate of torradas. When Mariana and Tiago walked in, Daniela stood up, waved, and moved a chair out for Mariana.

She signaled the waiter and said, “Could you bring one more chair, please?”

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The person participates in reciprocal visits, but treats them as obligations and acts uninterested, undermining the bonding intent.

GPT-5.2.name: Sérgio Mendes
GPT-5.2.story:
Sérgio kept a routine with his neighbors in Vila Real: one weekend at their place, the next at his. When it was his turn to host, he opened the door with his phone still in hand. “Come in,” he said, stepping aside without looking up.

His neighbors, Clara and Paulo, carried a small dessert tray. “We brought rice pudding,” Clara said.

Sérgio took it and set it on the counter. “Ok,” he replied, already typing a message. He pointed vaguely toward the living room. “Sit wherever you like.”

During the visit, he sat at the far end of the sofa, scrolling. Clara tried to start a conversation. “How was your week?”

“Normal,” Sérgio said, eyes still on the screen.

Paulo offered, “We could arrange a walk in the park tomorrow.”

Sérgio sighed quietly and tapped his calendar. “Yeah, if we have to,” he said.

When the coffee was ready, he set cups on the table without speaking and went back to his phone. Clara took a sip and looked around. Sérgio stood up after a short while and said, “Well, I have things to do.”

Paulo checked his watch. “We are leaving now.”

At the door, Sérgio gave a quick half-wave. “All right then, next week it is at your place,” he said.

He closed the door and immediately resumed typing on his phone in the hallway.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When visiting friends, the person accepts hospitality repeatedly but never engages in maintaining the relationship or responding to invitations.

GPT-5.2.name: Vítor Almeida
GPT-5.2.story:
Vítor stopped by his friend André’s place in Porto most weeks, usually around dinner time. André would say, “Sit down, there is always one more plate,” and set out food without hesitation. Vítor ate, watched TV, and left with a quick “see you later.”

One evening, André said, “One of these days you come here and then I will go to your house. We can have dinner there.”

“Yeah, we will arrange it,” Vítor replied, looking at his phone.

The next month, Vítor kept accepting invites. He showed up when André texted, “We are making sandwiches; come by.” Vítor arrived empty-handed, sat down, and ate. When André later sent, “So, when can I come by your house?”, Vítor didn’t answer that message.

A week later, André called. Vítor let it ring and then sent a short reply: “I was busy.” He still went to André’s place on Friday when he heard there would be bacalhau.

At the table, André mentioned again, “We could go have coffee on Sunday, you and I.”

“I will see later,” Vítor said, already reaching for the bread.

On Sunday, André sent a follow-up message with a time. Vítor read it and didn’t reply, then texted two days later: “Are you home today? I am hungry.”
